title: Cacalia cordigera
taxonomy: Cacalia cordigera is classified within the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This classification is based on its shared characteristics with other plants in the Asteraceae family, including its flower structure and seed production. Within the Asteraceae family, Cacalia cordigera is placed in the tribe Senecioneae, which is a group of plants that share similar morphological features. The species Cacalia cordigera is further classified as a member of the genus Cacalia, a group of plants that are primarily found in East Asia. Cacalia cordigera's classification is an example of the ongoing process of taxonomic classification and refinement in the field of botany.

intro: Cacalia cordigera, also known as Cordia, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Bignoniaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, with reported benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Cacalia cordigera include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Cacalia cordigera is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and ointments to promote wound healing and skin health.
